_comment: V3 Phase 1 (⑦) drift allowlist. Seeds the CURRENT production-B schema (VERIFIED 2026-07-15, docs/V3_SCHEMA_GAP_SQL_RESULTS.md) as the accepted baseline. Every entry is a known divergence between supabase_schema.sql / migration_*.sql (EXPECTED) and live production (ACTUAL) that the owner has accepted. The drift gate treats these as PASS. Phase 10 (⑧) formalizes each into an additive migration and removes it from this list. Nothing here is a secret; project ref aqozjkfwzmyfunqvcyjv is public.

columns.reason: drift column, 9 rows true; used by section/lesson UI
sync columns exist on courses (not only orders as V1 doc said)
V2 identity FK->courses.id (3 rows NULL, reconciliation pending)

tables.reason: V2 outbox applied partially; exists in catalog (RLS on, 0 policy), not exposed via REST
V2 outbox applied partially; exists in catalog (RLS on, 0 policy)
V2 outbox partial: NOT created on production; Phase 3 (④) adds it. Allowlisted so the gate does not fail on the absent third table until then
legacy projection living on same Supabase B (1 row); A/B ownership NOT yet decided by owner (GO condition #3)
PostGIS system table present in catalog

rls.reason: All 25 public tables have RLS ON with 0 policy and force-RLS OFF (VERIFIED). This is the accepted baseline; Phase 2 (①) writes real policies. RLS-on state itself is not drift — individual per-table RLS toggles are still gated as FAIL.

functions.reason: SECURITY INVOKER (not DEFINER) + proconfig null; accepted baseline, Phase 2 (①) normalizes to DEFINER + pinned search_path

grants.reason: service_role EXECUTE granted (PUBLIC/anon/authenticated correctly denied) — accepted baseline
